{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"haloperidol is a specific synthetic pharmaceutical compound identified by its chemical structure and neurochemical mechanism of action (dopamine D2 receptor antagonism). Its identity and therapeutic effects depend on precise molecular pharmacology — receptor binding, neurotransmitter dynamics, and clinical outcomes in psychiatric conditions — none of which are expressible in the prime lexicon or in grounding notes over the current catalog. It belongs to the technical-medical/chemical sector."}

<<<KOT:hematocrit>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"hematocrit is a quantitative biomedical measurement — a ratio (percentage by volume) of red blood cells in a blood sample, determined by centrifugation. Its identity and clinical meaning rest on cellular biology, blood physiology, and laboratory measurement standards. These cannot be grounded in the prime lexicon or catalog concepts; it belongs to the medical-laboratory science sector."}
